page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz, delivering 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong memory performance, with its Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25% tier. This is complemented by a high memory read-only cached bandwidth of 1699.39 GB/sec and uncached read bandwidth of 153.99 GB/sec. For computational workloads, the Intel Xeon processor utilizes advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI. The instance achieves average-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and Geekbench evaluations, though it shows strong text generation performance on smaller language models, processing 304.16 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource density make this instance a stable choice for workloads requiring consistent performance without the overhead of GPU hardware. The lack of bundled local storage requires external block storage, which should be factored into deployment architectures. The ecs.g7.8xlarge is suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, static web serving, database operations, and software compilation tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via a KVM hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but features a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory capabilities, highlighted by a top-tier Passmark memory score and efficient small-model LLM text generation. Supported by AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration, the ecs.g7.8xlarge is designed for enterprise applications, database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, strong memory performance, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth for enterprise workloads.

128 GB of RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
